readAloud.expectedAnswerFormat: Full text read aloud with natural pronunciation and fluency. No fixed expected answer; evaluated on content delivery, pronunciation, and fluency.

repeatSentence.expectedAnswerFormat: Exact repetition of the heard sentence. Evaluated on accuracy, pronunciation, and fluency.

describeImage.expectedAnswerFormat: Detailed description of the image covering key features, trends, and relationships. Evaluated on content, pronunciation, fluency, and structure.

retellLecture.expectedAnswerFormat: Summary of the lecture in own words covering main points. Evaluated on content, pronunciation, fluency, and structure.

answerShortQuestion.expectedAnswerFormat: Short, direct answer (1-3 words). See individual question answers.

summarizeWrittenText.expectedAnswerFormat: Single sentence summarizing the key points of the passage. See sample answers for each passage.

writeEssay.expectedAnswerFormat: Well-organized essay with clear position, supporting arguments, and examples. Evaluated on content, organization, grammar, vocabulary, and cohesion.

summarizeSpokenText.expectedAnswerFormat: Concise summary (50-70 words) covering the main points of the lecture. Evaluated on content, grammar, vocabulary, and conciseness.

tasks.prompt: Look at the text below. In 40 seconds, you must read this text aloud as naturally and as clearly as possible. You have 40 seconds to read aloud.
tasks.expectedAnswerFormat: Read the full text naturally and clearly.

items.title: Market research
items.text: Market research is a vital part of the planning of any business. However experienced you or your staff may be in a particular field, if you are thinking of introducing a service to a new area, it is important to find out what the local population thinks about it first.
items.expectedAnswer: Read aloud the provided text with clear pronunciation, appropriate pace, and natural intonation. No exact answer; evaluated on delivery.

items.title: Transportation of goods by water
items.text: Not a lot is known about how the transportation of goods by water first began. Large cargo boats were being used in some parts of the world up to five thousand years ago. However, sea trade became more widespread when large sailing boats travelled between ports, carrying spices, perfumes and objects made by hand.
items.expectedAnswer: Read aloud the provided text with clear pronunciation, appropriate pace, and natural intonation. No exact answer; evaluated on delivery.

items.title: Young artist
items.text: When the young artist was asked about his drawing, he explained that he had started by taking a photograph of himself sitting by a window at home. He then drew his face from the photograph and replaced the buildings which were outside the window with trees. This gave the picture a softer, more artistic background.
items.expectedAnswer: Read aloud the provided text with clear pronunciation, appropriate pace, and natural intonation. No exact answer; evaluated on delivery.

items.title: Energy and pollution
items.text: Humans need to use energy in order to exist. So it is unsurprising that the way people have been producing energy is largely responsible for current environmental problems. Pollution comes in many forms, but those that are most concerning, because of their impact on health, result from the combustion of fuels in power stations and cars.
items.expectedAnswer: Read aloud the provided text with clear pronunciation, appropriate pace, and natural intonation. No exact answer; evaluated on delivery.

items.title: Retirement
items.text: Clearly, times are changing and while many people are saving for their retirement, many more still need to do so. Most countries have a range of pension schemes that are designed to provide individuals with an income once they stop working. People need to take advantage of these if they are to have sufficient money throughout their retirement years.
items.expectedAnswer: Read aloud the provided text with clear pronunciation, appropriate pace, and natural intonation. No exact answer; evaluated on delivery.

items.title: Good weather and mood
items.text: According to recent research, sunshine and warm weather have a positive effect on our moods. The British Journal of Psychology has published a report in which it claims that anxiety levels fall when temperatures rise, while increased exposure to sunshine makes us think more positively about our lives.
items.expectedAnswer: Read aloud the provided text with clear pronunciation, appropriate pace, and natural intonation. No exact answer; evaluated on delivery.

items.expectedAnswer: Factors such as cost and function influence the design of a bridge.

items.expectedAnswer: It's important that humans dispose of their waste in appropriate ways.

items.expectedAnswer: If you are unable to complete the task in time please notify me by email.

items.expectedAnswer: The financial report for the last quarter will be available this afternoon.

items.sentence: Extra seminars will be scheduled to assist you with revision.
items.expectedAnswer: Extra seminars will be scheduled to assist you with revision.

items.expectedAnswer: Please switch off all electronic devices when you are attending a conference session.

items.sentence: It is important that you work as a team on this project.
items.expectedAnswer: It is important that you work as a team on this project.

items.sentence: The study showed that people's mood can be affected by news and weather reports.
items.expectedAnswer: The study showed that people's mood can be affected by news and weather reports.

items.sentence: Detailed analysis of population growth has revealed some alarming predictions.
items.expectedAnswer: Detailed analysis of population growth has revealed some alarming predictions.

items.sentence: Please note that the college laboratories will be closed for cleaning next week.
items.expectedAnswer: Please note that the college laboratories will be closed for cleaning next week.

tasks.prompt: Look at the [graph/charts/chart/graph/diagram] below. In 25 seconds, please speak into the microphone and describe in detail what the [graph/charts/chart/graph/diagram] is showing. You will have 40 seconds to give your response.
tasks.expectedAnswerFormat: Detailed description of the image: type of graph, axes/labels, key trends, comparisons, and conclusions.

items.title: Percentage world population by region
items.description: Line graph showing Asia and Europe from 1750 to 2000.
items.expectedAnswer: This line graph shows the percentage of world population in Asia and Europe from 1750 to 2000. Asia's population remained the largest throughout, while Europe's share declined. Both regions show fluctuations, with Asia peaking around 1950 and Europe steadily decreasing from 1900 onwards.

items.title: Reasons for no longer attending school
items.description: Two bar charts comparing reasons (completed study, obtained employment, personal/family, illness, other) for males and females.
items.expectedAnswer: The bar charts compare reasons for leaving school between males and females. For males, the main reasons are obtaining employment and completing study, while for females, personal/family reasons are more significant. Both genders show similar rates for illness and other reasons.

items.title: US citizens over 65 years
items.description: Line graph predicting percentage of US population aged 65+ from 2010 to 2035.
items.expectedAnswer: The line graph projects the percentage of US citizens aged 65 and over from 2010 to 2035. Starting at about 13% in 2010, it shows a steady increase to over 20% by 2035, with the most rapid growth occurring after 2020.

items.title: Revenue growth graph
items.description: Line graph showing projected revenue for Company X and Company Y from 2006 to 2024.
items.expectedAnswer: This line graph shows revenue projections for Company X and Company Y from 2006 to 2024. Company Y's revenue is consistently higher and shows more growth, increasing from approximately $40M to over $120M, while Company X grows from about $10M to $40M over the same period.

items.title: Reading achievement - Grade 1 to Grade 3
items.description: Line graph showing oral reading fluency (words per minute) for successful and struggling readers across three grades and seasons.
items.expectedAnswer: This line graph shows oral reading fluency for successful and struggling readers from Grade 1 to Grade 3. Successful readers show steady improvement, reaching about 110 words per minute by Grade 3, while struggling readers improve more slowly, reaching about 50 words per minute by Grade 3.

items.title: Shadouf - water collection
items.description: Diagram of a shadouf (a lever-and-bucket water collection system).
items.expectedAnswer: The diagram shows a shadouf, a traditional water collection system. It consists of a long pole balanced on a pivot, with a bucket attached to one end and a counterweight on the other. The system uses a lever principle to raise water from a well or water source.

tasks.prompt: You will hear a lecture. After listening to the lecture, in 10 seconds, please speak into the microphone and retell what you have just heard from the lecture in your own words. You will have 40 seconds to give your response.
tasks.expectedAnswerFormat: Oral summary of the lecture in own words covering main ideas and key details.

items.title: Air pollution
items.audioScriptSummary: Changes in air pollution from 1950s (visible smog from factories) to today (invisible pollution from vehicles); Clean Air Act of 1956; 1952 London smog caused 4,000 deaths.
items.expectedAnswer: The lecture discusses the evolution of air pollution. In the 1950s, visible smog from factories was the main concern, but today, invisible pollution from vehicles is the primary issue. The Clean Air Act of 1956 was introduced following the 1952 London smog, which caused around 4,000 deaths. The speaker emphasizes the shift from visible to invisible pollutants.

items.title: Focus Groups
items.audioScriptSummary: Tips on using focus groups: ideal size (6-7 people), need a moderator, use materials (pictures, diagrams) to start discussion.
items.expectedAnswer: The lecture provides tips for conducting effective focus groups. The ideal group size is 6-7 participants, and a skilled moderator is essential to guide the discussion. Using visual materials like pictures and diagrams can help initiate and stimulate conversation among participants.

items.title: Setting up a website
items.audioScriptSummary: Three key points: define target audience, ensure accessibility (links/social media), and retain users by keeping content up-to-date.
items.expectedAnswer: The lecture outlines three key considerations for setting up a successful website. First, define your target audience to tailor content appropriately. Second, ensure accessibility through functional links and social media integration. Third, retain users by keeping the website content fresh and up-to-date.

items.question: If you don't feel like eating, what do we say you don't have?
items.expectedAnswer: appetite

tasks.prompt: Read the passage below and summarize it using one sentence. Type your response in the box at the bottom of the screen. You have 10 minutes to finish this task. Your response will be judged on the quality of your writing and on how well your response presents the key points in the passage.
tasks.expectedAnswerFormat: One sentence summarizing the key points of the passage.

items.title: Tea
items.text: Full passage about tea as the second most popular drink, its growing conditions, and its worldwide export and variety.
items.expectedAnswer: Tea, the world's second most popular beverage, requires specific growing conditions including high altitudes, tropical climates, and well-drained soil, with its global export encompassing numerous varieties across different regions.

items.title: Over-fishing
items.text: Passage about increased fish consumption, overfishing, declining catches, and the need for conservation.
items.expectedAnswer: The growing demand for fish has led to overfishing and declining catches, necessitating urgent conservation measures to ensure the sustainability of fish stocks for future generations.

tasks.prompt: You will have 20 minutes to plan, write and revise an essay about the topic below. Your response will be judged on how well you develop a position, organize your ideas, present supporting details, and control the elements of standard written English. You should write 200-300 words.
tasks.expectedAnswerFormat: Well-structured essay with clear introduction, body paragraphs, and conclusion.

items.topic: As a result of advances in medical care, average life expectancy is increasing for men and women. Do you think most people will see this as a positive development? What are the disadvantages of an ageing population for individuals and society? Support your point of view with reasons and/or examples from your own experience or observations.
items.expectedAnswer: Sample essay structure: Introduction - acknowledge advances and present balanced view. Body paragraph 1 - discuss positive aspects (longer time with family, more wisdom, extended careers). Body paragraph 2 - discuss disadvantages (healthcare costs, pension pressure, workforce shortage). Body paragraph 3 - personal experience/observation. Conclusion - weigh benefits against challenges, suggest balanced approach.

items.topic: 'Schools should prepare students for university, rather than for work.' How far do you agree with this statement? Support your point of view with reasons and/or examples from your own experience or observations.
items.expectedAnswer: Sample essay structure: Introduction - present the debate and state position. Body paragraph 1 - arguments for university preparation (academic skills, critical thinking, research abilities). Body paragraph 2 - arguments for work preparation (practical skills, employability, vocational training). Body paragraph 3 - personal experience/observations. Conclusion - propose balanced approach between university and work preparation.

tasks.prompt: Read the text and answer the multiple-choice question by selecting the correct response. Only one response is correct.

items.title: Geothermal power in Britain
items.text: Passage about geothermal energy potential in Britain, its benefits and challenges.

options: A) to show how common the use of geothermal power is in Britain
B) to describe the problems related to using geothermal power in Britain
C) to highlight the potential benefits of geothermal power for Britain
D) to give a detailed description of how geothermal energy is produced in Britain
items.expectedAnswer: C

items.title: Innovation

options: A) Many new products fail to interest consumers.
B) New products are not always based on new ideas.
C) Creators of new products require a unique set of skills.
D) New products are easy to distinguish from old ones.
items.expectedAnswer: B

B) It is not present in rainforests.
C) It has resulted from agricultural activity.

E) It is being found near where humans have lived.

G) It is less productive than people once thought.

tasks.prompt: The text boxes in the left panel have been placed in a random order. Restore the original order by dragging the text boxes from the left panel to the right panel.

items.title: Grand Canyon

sentences.A: Clearly, a number of factors have contributed to its remarkable appearance.
sentences.B: The result is a unique story of land collisions and erosions, and of rising and falling water levels.
sentences.C: Experts who have analysed the rock formations say that, historically, it goes back nearly two billion years.
sentences.D: Anyone who has ever visited the Grand Canyon will agree that it is one of the most incredible sights in the world.
sentences.E: The geological processes that have taken place since then are exposed for everyone to see, not hidden beneath vegetation or a fast-flowing water course.

items.expectedAnswer: D → A → C → E → B

items.title: Organic farming

sentences.A: It was a time when managers had to take a critical look at every aspect of their production process and make improvements where necessary.
sentences.B: As a result, some people believe it is now time to re-assess many companies in terms of the standards they agreed to some years ago.
sentences.C: In the late 1900s, food manufacturers were challenged by the organic community to ensure they were using ingredients that had been produced in natural, healthy ways.
sentences.D: Whether these systems have been maintained seems questionable, particularly as contracts depend so heavily on efficiency and quick sales.
sentences.E: Over the last half-century, organic farming has become a driving force in the world's food market.

items.expectedAnswer: E → C → A → D → B

tasks.prompt: You will hear a short lecture. Write a summary for a fellow student who was not present at the lecture. You should write 50-70 words.
tasks.expectedAnswerFormat: Concise summary (50-70 words) covering the main points.

items.title: Technological nature
items.topic: Comparing real nature views with computer-generated virtual nature; experiment shows real views reduce stress faster.
items.expectedAnswer: The lecture compares real nature views with virtual nature experiences. An experiment showed that while both can reduce stress, real nature views are more effective and reduce stress levels faster than computer-generated alternatives. This suggests that authentic natural environments have unique restorative benefits.

items.title: Memories in criminal trials
items.topic: Memory unreliability in court; new information can distort witness memory; research with video and added details.
items.expectedAnswer: The lecture discusses the unreliability of memory in criminal trials. Research shows that new information can distort witness memories, making them unreliable. An experiment using video footage demonstrated that adding details after the event can significantly alter what witnesses recall, raising concerns about the use of memory in legal proceedings.

The first migratory routes were established thousands of years ago.
